name: Intersection Observer API
performance_characteristics: Asynchronous, off-main-thread visibility checks; vastly cheaper than scroll-event polling and getBoundingClientRect loops
unsuitable_contexts: sub-pixel continuous scroll progress tracking (use scroll-driven animations instead)
strengths: Baseline everywhere; the standard tool for lazy-loading, infinite scroll and reveal-on-scroll
Eliminates expensive scroll listeners and forced layout reflows
Configurable thresholds and rootMargin for precise trigger control
Low battery and CPU cost, safe on low-power and mobile devices
weaknesses: Reports discrete threshold crossings, not continuous scroll position
Callbacks are async, so it is not suited to per-frame animation driving
Observing huge numbers of targets still carries some bookkeeping cost
